text.title: EVM Rules and Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Rules for late-project accuracy, risk linkage, and agentic EVM workflow; anti-patterns that destroy EVM credibility.

rule.statement: Use SPI(t) (time-based Schedule Performance Index) alongside classic SPI for late-stage reporting; classic SPI converges to 1.0 as PV approaches BAC regardless of actual schedule status.
rule.rationale: In the final 20% of a project, PV and BAC are nearly equal; SV and SPI lose discrimination and can show "on schedule" for a project that will be weeks late. SPI(t) = ES / AT remains meaningful throughout.

rule.statement: Triangulate index-based EAC against a bottom-up ETC at least quarterly; pure CPI-based forecasts drift over long durations.
rule.rationale: Index-based forecasts assume the current efficiency persists unchanged; a bottom-up re-estimate incorporates changed scope, team, or technical complexity that the indices cannot see.

rule.statement: Tie every RED index (SPI < 0.85 or CPI < 0.85) to at least one open risk in the risk register with an explicit action plan; an unexplained RED is an audit finding.
rule.rationale: Red performance without a linked risk and response plan is noise; linking to the register forces the team to decide: accept, mitigate, transfer, or escalate — and documents the decision.

rule.statement: When a risk closes, verify that the associated performance indices improve in the next period; if they do not, the root cause was misdiagnosed.
rule.rationale: Closing risks without verifying performance impact creates a false sense of recovery; unverified closures leave real causes unaddressed.

rule.statement: The EVM narrative agent receives typed numeric inputs and emits a RAG verdict by rule (GREEN: SPI >= 0.95 and CPI >= 0.95; RED: SPI < 0.85 or CPI < 0.85; YELLOW: otherwise); it must not soften RED without an explicit sponsor-override field.
rule.rationale: Without a hard RAG rule, narrative agents anchor toward "improving" language even for persistent red indices, which misleads sponsors and delays interventions.

antipattern.description: Letting the LLM compute EVM numbers from narrative inputs (e.g., "we spent about $55k and are roughly 40% done").
antipattern.reason: Narrative inputs introduce ambiguity; LLMs round inconsistently and may interpolate missing values. Only typed numeric inputs from the accounting system and objective DoD assessments are valid.

antipattern.description: Reporting only one EAC scenario in the sponsor update.
antipattern.reason: A single EAC anchors expectations on the most likely (or most optimistic) case; three scenarios expose the range of risk and prevent overconfidence.
